authors_text: Gyungin Shin,Weidi Xie,Samuel Albanie
title: ReCo: Retrieve and Co-segment for Zero-shot Transfer
title_zh: ReCo：零样本传输的检索和联合分段
abstract: Semantic segmentation has a broad range of applications, but its real-world impact has been significantly limited by the prohibitive annotation costs necessary to enable deployment. Segmentation methods that forgo supervision can side-step these costs, but exhibit the inconvenient requirement to provide labelled examples from the target distribution to assign concept names to predictions. An alternative line of work in language-image pre-training has recently demonstrated the potential to produce models that can both assign names across large vocabularies of concepts and enable zero-shot transfer for classification, but do not demonstrate commensurate segmentation abilities. In this work, we strive to achieve a synthesis of these two approaches that combines their strengths. We leverage the retrieval abilities of one such language-image pre-trained model, CLIP, to dynamically curate training sets from unlabelled images for arbitrary collections of concept names, and leverage the robust correspondences offered by modern image representations to co-segment entities among the resulting collections. The synthetic segment collections are then employed to construct a segmentation model (without requiring pixel labels) whose knowledge of concepts is inherited from the scalable pre-training process of CLIP. We demonstrate that our approach, termed Retrieve and Co-segment (ReCo) performs favourably to unsupervised segmentation approaches while inheriting the convenience of nameable predictions and zero-shot transfer. We also demonstrate ReCo's ability to generate specialist segmenters for extremely rare objects.
summary: Semantic segmentation has a broad range of applications, but its real-world impact has been significantly limited by the prohibitive annotation costs necessary to enable deployment. Segmentation methods that forgo supervision can side-step these costs, but exhibit the inconvenient requirement to provide labelled examples from the target distribution to assign concept names to predictions. An alternative line of work in language-image pre-training has recently demonstrated the potential to produce models that can both assign names across large vocabularies of concepts and enable zero-shot transfer for classification, but do not demonstrate commensurate segmentation abilities. In this work, we strive to achieve a synthesis of these two approaches that combines their strengths. We leverage the retrieval abilities of one such language-image pre-trained model, CLIP, to dynamically curate training sets from unlabelled images for arbitrary collections of concept names, and leverage the robust correspondences offered by modern image representations to co-segment entities among the resulting collections. The synthetic segment collections are then employed to construct a segmentation model (without requiring pixel labels) whose knowledge of concepts is inherited from the scalable pre-training process of CLIP. We demonstrate that our approach, termed Retrieve and Co-segment (ReCo) performs favourably to unsupervised segmentation approaches while inheriting the convenience of nameable predictions and zero-shot transfer. We also demonstrate ReCo's ability to generate specialist segmenters for extremely rare objects.
abstract_zh: 语义分割具有广泛的应用，但其实际影响因部署所需的高昂注释成本而受到严重限制。放弃监督的分割方法可以回避这些成本，但需要提供目标分布中的标记示例以将概念名称分配给预测，这会带来不便。语言图像预训练中的另一项工作最近证明了产生模型的潜力，该模型既可以在大量概念词汇中分配名称，又可以实现分类的零样本迁移，但没有表现出相应的分割能力。在这项工作中，我们努力实现这两种方法的综合，结合它们的优势。我们利用这样一种语言图像预训练模型 CLIP 的检索能力，从未标记的图像中动态地管理训练集，以获取任意概念名称集合，并利用现代图像表示提供的强大对应关系来对结果集合中的实体进行共同分割。然后使用合成片段集合来构建分割模型（不需要像素标签），其概念知识继承自 CLIP 的可扩展预训练过程。我们证明了我们的方法，称为检索和联合分割（ReCo），其性能优于无监督分割方法，同时继承了可命名预测和零样本传输的便利性。我们还展示了 ReCo 为极其稀有物体生成专业分割器的能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ReCo方法，通过结合语言-图像预训练模型的检索能力和图像表示的协同分割，实现无需像素标注的零样本语义分割。
AI.motivation: 现有语义分割方法依赖昂贵标注，无监督方法需目标分布标记示例，而预训练模型虽具零样本分类能力但缺乏分割能力，需融合两者优势。
AI.method: 利用CLIP模型检索能力动态生成概念名称的训练集，通过现代图像表示建立实体协同分割，构建继承CLIP预训练知识的分割模型。
AI.result: ReCo在无监督分割方法中表现优异，支持可命名预测与零样本迁移，并能生成针对稀有物体的专用分割器。
AI.conclusion: ReCo有效整合了预训练模型的可扩展性与协同分割优势，推动了零样本语义分割的发展。

search_text: ReCo: Retrieve and Co-segment for Zero-shot Transfer ReCo：零样本传输的检索和联合分段 Semantic segmentation has a broad range of applications, but its real-world impact has been significantly limited by the prohibitive annotation costs necessary to enable deployment. Segmentation methods that forgo supervision can side-step these costs, but exhibit the inconvenient requirement to provide labelled examples from the target distribution to assign concept names to predictions. An alternative line of work in language-image pre-training has recently demonstrated the potential to produce models that can both assign names across large vocabularies of concepts and enable zero-shot transfer for classification, but do not demonstrate commensurate segmentation abilities. In this work, we strive to achieve a synthesis of these two approaches that combines their strengths. We leverage the retrieval abilities of one such language-image pre-trained model, CLIP, to dynamically curate training sets from unlabelled images for arbitrary collections of concept names, and leverage the robust correspondences offered by modern image representations to co-segment entities among the resulting collections. The synthetic segment collections are then employed to construct a segmentation model (without requiring pixel labels) whose knowledge of concepts is inherited from the scalable pre-training process of CLIP. We demonstrate that our approach, termed Retrieve and Co-segment (ReCo) performs favourably to unsupervised segmentation approaches while inheriting the convenience of nameable predictions and zero-shot transfer. We also demonstrate ReCo's ability to generate specialist segmenters for extremely rare objects. 语义分割具有广泛的应用，但其实际影响因部署所需的高昂注释成本而受到严重限制。放弃监督的分割方法可以回避这些成本，但需要提供目标分布中的标记示例以将概念名称分配给预测，这会带来不便。语言图像预训练中的另一项工作最近证明了产生模型的潜力，该模型既可以在大量概念词汇中分配名称，又可以实现分类的零样本迁移，但没有表现出相应的分割能力。在这项工作中，我们努力实现这两种方法的综合，结合它们的优势。我们利用这样一种语言图像预训练模型 CLIP 的检索能力，从未标记的图像中动态地管理训练集，以获取任意概念名称集合，并利用现代图像表示提供的强大对应关系来对结果集合中的实体进行共同分割。然后使用合成片段集合来构建分割模型（不需要像素标签），其概念知识继承自 CLIP 的可扩展预训练过程。我们证明了我们的方法，称为检索和联合分割（ReCo），其性能优于无监督分割方法，同时继承了可命名预测和零样本传输的便利性。我们还展示了 ReCo 为极其稀有物体生成专业分割器的能力。 本文提出ReCo方法，通过结合语言-图像预训练模型的检索能力和图像表示的协同分割，实现无需像素标注的零样本语义分割。 Gyungin Shin,Weidi Xie,Samuel Albanie cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Yanze Wu,Xintao Wang,Gen Li,Ying Shan
title: AnimeSR: Learning Real-World Super-Resolution Models for Animation Videos
title_zh: AnimeSR：学习动画视频的真实世界超分辨率模型
comment: NeurIPS 2022. Codes and models are available at https://github.com/TencentARC/AnimeSR
comments: NeurIPS 2022. Codes and models are available at https://github.com/TencentARC/AnimeSR
abstract: This paper studies the problem of real-world video super-resolution (VSR) for animation videos, and reveals three key improvements for practical animation VSR. First, recent real-world super-resolution approaches typically rely on degradation simulation using basic operators without any learning capability, such as blur, noise, and compression. In this work, we propose to learn such basic operators from real low-quality animation videos, and incorporate the learned ones into the degradation generation pipeline. Such neural-network-based basic operators could help to better capture the distribution of real degradations. Second, a large-scale high-quality animation video dataset, AVC, is built to facilitate comprehensive training and evaluations for animation VSR. Third, we further investigate an efficient multi-scale network structure. It takes advantage of the efficiency of unidirectional recurrent networks and the effectiveness of sliding-window-based methods. Thanks to the above delicate designs, our method, AnimeSR, is capable of restoring real-world low-quality animation videos effectively and efficiently, achieving superior performance to previous state-of-the-art methods. Codes and models are available at https://github.com/TencentARC/AnimeSR.
summary: This paper studies the problem of real-world video super-resolution (VSR) for animation videos, and reveals three key improvements for practical animation VSR. First, recent real-world super-resolution approaches typically rely on degradation simulation using basic operators without any learning capability, such as blur, noise, and compression. In this work, we propose to learn such basic operators from real low-quality animation videos, and incorporate the learned ones into the degradation generation pipeline. Such neural-network-based basic operators could help to better capture the distribution of real degradations. Second, a large-scale high-quality animation video dataset, AVC, is built to facilitate comprehensive training and evaluations for animation VSR. Third, we further investigate an efficient multi-scale network structure. It takes advantage of the efficiency of unidirectional recurrent networks and the effectiveness of sliding-window-based methods. Thanks to the above delicate designs, our method, AnimeSR, is capable of restoring real-world low-quality animation videos effectively and efficiently, achieving superior performance to previous state-of-the-art methods. Codes and models are available at https://github.com/TencentARC/AnimeSR.
abstract_zh: 本文研究了动画视频的现实世界视频超分辨率（VSR）问题，并揭示了实用动画 VSR 的三个关键改进。首先，最近的现实世界超分辨率方法通常依赖于使用基本算子的退化模拟，而没有任何学习能力，例如模糊、噪声和压缩。在这项工作中，我们建议从真实的低质量动画视频中学习此类基本运算符，并将学习到的运算符合并到退化生成管道中。这种基于神经网络的基本算子可以帮助更好地捕获真实退化的分布。其次，建立大规模高质量动画视频数据集AVC，以方便动画VSR的综合训练和评估。第三，我们进一步研究有效的多尺度网络结构。它利用了单向循环网络的效率和基于滑动窗口的方法的有效性。由于上述精心设计，我们的方法 AnimeSR 能够有效且高效地恢复现实世界的低质量动画视频，实现优于以前最先进方法的性能。代码和模型可在 https://github.com/TencentARC/AnimeSR 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文研究真实世界动画视频的超分辨率问题，提出AnimeSR方法以实现高效高质量的动画视频超分辨率。
AI.motivation: 传统超分辨率方法依赖无学习能力的退化模拟操作符，难以准确捕捉真实退化分布，且缺乏针对动画视频的专用数据集和高效网络结构。
AI.method: 提出三点改进：1）从真实低质动画视频中学习退化操作符；2）构建大规模高质量动画视频数据集AVC；3）设计结合单向循环网络与滑动窗口的多尺度高效网络结构。
AI.result: AnimeSR在多个基准测试中优于现有最先进方法，能有效恢复真实低质动画视频，代码和模型已开源。
AI.conclusion: 通过真实退化建模、专用数据集和高效网络设计，AnimeSR实现了动画视频超分辨率的性能突破，为实际应用提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Vasileios Choutas,Lea Muller,Chun-Hao P. Huang,Siyu Tang,Dimitrios Tzionas,Michael J. Black
title: Accurate 3D Body Shape Regression using Metric and Semantic Attributes
title_zh: 使用度量和语义属性进行准确的 3D 体形回归
abstract: While methods that regress 3D human meshes from images have progressed rapidly, the estimated body shapes often do not capture the true human shape. This is problematic since, for many applications, accurate body shape is as important as pose. The key reason that body shape accuracy lags pose accuracy is the lack of data. While humans can label 2D joints, and these constrain 3D pose, it is not so easy to "label" 3D body shape. Since paired data with images and 3D body shape are rare, we exploit two sources of information: (1) we collect internet images of diverse "fashion" models together with a small set of anthropometric measurements; (2) we collect linguistic shape attributes for a wide range of 3D body meshes and the model images. Taken together, these datasets provide sufficient constraints to infer dense 3D shape. We exploit the anthropometric measurements and linguistic shape attributes in several novel ways to train a neural network, called SHAPY, that regresses 3D human pose and shape from an RGB image. We evaluate SHAPY on public benchmarks, but note that they either lack significant body shape variation, ground-truth shape, or clothing variation. Thus, we collect a new dataset for evaluating 3D human shape estimation, called HBW, containing photos of "Human Bodies in the Wild" for which we have ground-truth 3D body scans. On this new benchmark, SHAPY significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods on the task of 3D body shape estimation. This is the first demonstration that 3D body shape regression from images can be trained from easy-to-obtain anthropometric measurements and linguistic shape attributes. Our model and data are available at: shapy.is.tue.mpg.de
summary: While methods that regress 3D human meshes from images have progressed rapidly, the estimated body shapes often do not capture the true human shape. This is problematic since, for many applications, accurate body shape is as important as pose. The key reason that body shape accuracy lags pose accuracy is the lack of data. While humans can label 2D joints, and these constrain 3D pose, it is not so easy to "label" 3D body shape. Since paired data with images and 3D body shape are rare, we exploit two sources of information: (1) we collect internet images of diverse "fashion" models together with a small set of anthropometric measurements; (2) we collect linguistic shape attributes for a wide range of 3D body meshes and the model images. Taken together, these datasets provide sufficient constraints to infer dense 3D shape. We exploit the anthropometric measurements and linguistic shape attributes in several novel ways to train a neural network, called SHAPY, that regresses 3D human pose and shape from an RGB image. We evaluate SHAPY on public benchmarks, but note that they either lack significant body shape variation, ground-truth shape, or clothing variation. Thus, we collect a new dataset for evaluating 3D human shape estimation, called HBW, containing photos of "Human Bodies in the Wild" for which we have ground-truth 3D body scans. On this new benchmark, SHAPY significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods on the task of 3D body shape estimation. This is the first demonstration that 3D body shape regression from images can be trained from easy-to-obtain anthropometric measurements and linguistic shape attributes. Our model and data are available at: shapy.is.tue.mpg.de
abstract_zh: 虽然从图像中回归 3D 人体网格的方法进展迅速，但估计的身体形状通常无法捕捉真实的人体形状。这是有问题的，因为对于许多应用来说，准确的身体形状与姿势一样重要。体形精度落后于姿势精度的关键原因是缺乏数据。虽然人类可以标记 2D 关节，并且这些关节限制了 3D 姿势，但“标记”3D 身体形状并不那么容易。由于图像和 3D 体型的配对数据很少，我们利用两种信息来源：(1) 我们收集各种“时尚”模特的互联网图像以及一小组人体测量数据； (2) 我们收集各种 3D 身体网格和模型图像的语言形状属性。总而言之，这些数据集提供了足够的约束来推断密集的 3D 形状。我们以多种新颖的方式利用人体测量测量和语言形状属性来训练称为 SHAPY 的神经网络，该网络可以从 RGB 图像中回归 3D 人体姿势和形状。我们在公共基准上评估 SHAPY，但注意到它们要么缺乏显着的体型变化、真实形状或服装变化。因此，我们收集了一个用于评估 3D 人体形状估计的新数据集，称为 HBW，其中包含“野外人体”的照片，我们对其进行了地面实况 3D 人体扫描。在这个新基准上，SHAPY 在 3D 体形估计任务上显着优于最先进的方法。这是首次证明可以通过易于获得的人体测量值和语言形状属性来训练图像的 3D 体形回归。我们的模型和数据可在以下网址获取：shapy.is.tue.mpg.de

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种利用度量属性和语义属性进行图像到3D人体形状的准确回归方法。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在从图像回归3D人体网格时精度不足，因缺乏标注3D形状的数据。人体姿态标注易得但形状标注困难，需探索新数据源提升精度。
AI.method: 收集带人体测量数据的时尚模特图像及3D网格的语义属性描述，训练神经网络SHAPY，结合度量和语义属性实现姿态与形状联合回归，并构建含真实3D扫描数据的HBW数据集进行评估。
AI.result: SHAPY在HBW数据集上显著优于现有方法，首次证明可通过易获取的测量数据和语义属性训练出高精度3D人体形状回归模型。
AI.conclusion: 通过融合度量属性与语义信息，结合新型数据集，实现了更准确的3D人体形状估计，为相关研究提供了新方法和基准数据。

ai_content_json.conclusion: 通过融合度量属性与语义信息，结合新型数据集，实现了更准确的3D人体形状估计，为相关研究提供了新方法和基准数据。

tldr: 本文提出一种利用度量属性和语义属性进行图像到3D人体形状的准确回归方法。
motivation: 现有方法在从图像回归3D人体网格时精度不足，因缺乏标注3D形状的数据。人体姿态标注易得但形状标注困难，需探索新数据源提升精度。
method: 收集带人体测量数据的时尚模特图像及3D网格的语义属性描述，训练神经网络SHAPY，结合度量和语义属性实现姿态与形状联合回归，并构建含真实3D扫描数据的HBW数据集进行评估。
result: SHAPY在HBW数据集上显著优于现有方法，首次证明可通过易获取的测量数据和语义属性训练出高精度3D人体形状回归模型。
conclusion: 通过融合度量属性与语义信息，结合新型数据集，实现了更准确的3D人体形状估计，为相关研究提供了新方法和基准数据。
search_text: Accurate 3D Body Shape Regression using Metric and Semantic Attributes 使用度量和语义属性进行准确的 3D 体形回归 While methods that regress 3D human meshes from images have progressed rapidly, the estimated body shapes often do not capture the true human shape. This is problematic since, for many applications, accurate body shape is as important as pose. The key reason that body shape accuracy lags pose accuracy is the lack of data. While humans can label 2D joints, and these constrain 3D pose, it is not so easy to "label" 3D body shape. Since paired data with images and 3D body shape are rare, we exploit two sources of information: (1) we collect internet images of diverse "fashion" models together with a small set of anthropometric measurements; (2) we collect linguistic shape attributes for a wide range of 3D body meshes and the model images. Taken together, these datasets provide sufficient constraints to infer dense 3D shape. We exploit the anthropometric measurements and linguistic shape attributes in several novel ways to train a neural network, called SHAPY, that regresses 3D human pose and shape from an RGB image. We evaluate SHAPY on public benchmarks, but note that they either lack significant body shape variation, ground-truth shape, or clothing variation. Thus, we collect a new dataset for evaluating 3D human shape estimation, called HBW, containing photos of "Human Bodies in the Wild" for which we have ground-truth 3D body scans. On this new benchmark, SHAPY significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods on the task of 3D body shape estimation. This is the first demonstration that 3D body shape regression from images can be trained from easy-to-obtain anthropometric measurements and linguistic shape attributes. Our model and data are available at: shapy.is.tue.mpg.de 虽然从图像中回归 3D 人体网格的方法进展迅速，但估计的身体形状通常无法捕捉真实的人体形状。这是有问题的，因为对于许多应用来说，准确的身体形状与姿势一样重要。体形精度落后于姿势精度的关键原因是缺乏数据。虽然人类可以标记 2D 关节，并且这些关节限制了 3D 姿势，但“标记”3D 身体形状并不那么容易。由于图像和 3D 体型的配对数据很少，我们利用两种信息来源：(1) 我们收集各种“时尚”模特的互联网图像以及一小组人体测量数据； (2) 我们收集各种 3D 身体网格和模型图像的语言形状属性。总而言之，这些数据集提供了足够的约束来推断密集的 3D 形状。我们以多种新颖的方式利用人体测量测量和语言形状属性来训练称为 SHAPY 的神经网络，该网络可以从 RGB 图像中回归 3D 人体姿势和形状。我们在公共基准上评估 SHAPY，但注意到它们要么缺乏显着的体型变化、真实形状或服装变化。因此，我们收集了一个用于评估 3D 人体形状估计的新数据集，称为 HBW，其中包含“野外人体”的照片，我们对其进行了地面实况 3D 人体扫描。在这个新基准上，SHAPY 在 3D 体形估计任务上显着优于最先进的方法。这是首次证明可以通过易于获得的人体测量值和语言形状属性来训练图像的 3D 体形回归。我们的模型和数据可在以下网址获取：shapy.is.tue.mpg.de 本文提出一种利用度量属性和语义属性进行图像到3D人体形状的准确回归方法。 Vasileios Choutas,Lea Muller,Chun-Hao P. Huang,Siyu Tang,Dimitrios Tzionas,Michael J. Black cs.CV

authors_text: Georgia Gkioxari,Nikhila Ravi,Justin Johnson
title: Learning 3D Object Shape and Layout without 3D Supervision
title_zh: 在没有 3D 监督的情况下学习 3D 对象形状和布局
abstract: A 3D scene consists of a set of objects, each with a shape and a layout giving their position in space. Understanding 3D scenes from 2D images is an important goal, with applications in robotics and graphics. While there have been recent advances in predicting 3D shape and layout from a single image, most approaches rely on 3D ground truth for training which is expensive to collect at scale. We overcome these limitations and propose a method that learns to predict 3D shape and layout for objects without any ground truth shape or layout information: instead we rely on multi-view images with 2D supervision which can more easily be collected at scale. Through extensive experiments on 3D Warehouse, Hypersim, and ScanNet we demonstrate that our approach scales to large datasets of realistic images, and compares favorably to methods relying on 3D ground truth. On Hypersim and ScanNet where reliable 3D ground truth is not available, our approach outperforms supervised approaches trained on smaller and less diverse datasets.
summary: A 3D scene consists of a set of objects, each with a shape and a layout giving their position in space. Understanding 3D scenes from 2D images is an important goal, with applications in robotics and graphics. While there have been recent advances in predicting 3D shape and layout from a single image, most approaches rely on 3D ground truth for training which is expensive to collect at scale. We overcome these limitations and propose a method that learns to predict 3D shape and layout for objects without any ground truth shape or layout information: instead we rely on multi-view images with 2D supervision which can more easily be collected at scale. Through extensive experiments on 3D Warehouse, Hypersim, and ScanNet we demonstrate that our approach scales to large datasets of realistic images, and compares favorably to methods relying on 3D ground truth. On Hypersim and ScanNet where reliable 3D ground truth is not available, our approach outperforms supervised approaches trained on smaller and less diverse datasets.
abstract_zh: 3D 场景由一组对象组成，每个对象都有一个形状和一个布局，指定它们在空间中的位置。从 2D 图像中理解 3D 场景是一个重要的目标，在机器人和图形学中都有应用。虽然最近在从单个图像预测 3D 形状和布局方面取得了进展，但大多数方法都依赖于 3D 地面实况进行训练，而大规模收集的成本很高。我们克服了这些限制，并提出了一种方法，可以学习预测对象的 3D 形状和布局，而无需任何地面真实形状或布局信息：相反，我们依赖具有 2D 监督的多视图图像，可以更轻松地大规模收集。通过在 3D Warehouse、Hypersim 和 ScanNet 上进行的大量实验，我们证明了我们的方法可以扩展到真实图像的大型数据集，并且与依赖 3D 地面实况的方法相比具有优势。在无法获得可靠的 3D 地面实况的 Hypersim 和 ScanNet 上，我们的方法优于在较小且不太多样化的数据集上训练的监督方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需3D监督信息即可学习3D物体形状和布局的方法。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖昂贵的3D真实数据进行训练，而本文旨在通过多视角2D图像和2D监督实现更易扩展的3D场景理解。
AI.method: 利用多视角图像与2D监督信号进行训练，无需任何3D形状或布局的真值数据。
AI.result: 在3D Warehouse、Hypersim和ScanNet数据集上验证了方法的有效性，尤其在缺乏可靠3D真值的Hypersim和ScanNet上优于基于小规模数据集的监督方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法成功实现了无需3D监督的3D场景理解，具有更好的可扩展性和实用性。

authors_text: Juri Opitz,Anette Frank
title: SBERT studies Meaning Representations: Decomposing Sentence Embeddings into Explainable Semantic Features
title_zh: SBERT 研究意义表示：将句子嵌入分解为可解释的语义特征
abstract: Models based on large-pretrained language models, such as S(entence)BERT, provide effective and efficient sentence embeddings that show high correlation to human similarity ratings, but lack interpretability. On the other hand, graph metrics for graph-based meaning representations (e.g., Abstract Meaning Representation, AMR) can make explicit the semantic aspects in which two sentences are similar. However, such metrics tend to be slow, rely on parsers, and do not reach state-of-the-art performance when rating sentence similarity. In this work, we aim at the best of both worlds, by learning to induce $S$emantically $S$tructured $S$entence BERT embeddings (S$^3$BERT). Our S$^3$BERT embeddings are composed of explainable sub-embeddings that emphasize various semantic sentence features (e.g., semantic roles, negation, or quantification). We show how to i) learn a decomposition of the sentence embeddings into semantic features, through approximation of a suite of interpretable AMR graph metrics, and how to ii) preserve the overall power of the neural embeddings by controlling the decomposition learning process with a second objective that enforces consistency with the similarity ratings of an SBERT teacher model. In our experimental studies, we show that our approach offers interpretability -- while fully preserving the effectiveness and efficiency of the neural sentence embeddings.
summary: Models based on large-pretrained language models, such as S(entence)BERT, provide effective and efficient sentence embeddings that show high correlation to human similarity ratings, but lack interpretability. On the other hand, graph metrics for graph-based meaning representations (e.g., Abstract Meaning Representation, AMR) can make explicit the semantic aspects in which two sentences are similar. However, such metrics tend to be slow, rely on parsers, and do not reach state-of-the-art performance when rating sentence similarity. In this work, we aim at the best of both worlds, by learning to induce $S$emantically $S$tructured $S$entence BERT embeddings (S$^3$BERT). Our S$^3$BERT embeddings are composed of explainable sub-embeddings that emphasize various semantic sentence features (e.g., semantic roles, negation, or quantification). We show how to i) learn a decomposition of the sentence embeddings into semantic features, through approximation of a suite of interpretable AMR graph metrics, and how to ii) preserve the overall power of the neural embeddings by controlling the decomposition learning process with a second objective that enforces consistency with the similarity ratings of an SBERT teacher model. In our experimental studies, we show that our approach offers interpretability -- while fully preserving the effectiveness and efficiency of the neural sentence embeddings.
abstract_zh: 基于大型预训练语言模型的模型，例如 S(entence)BERT，提供了有效且高效的句子嵌入，与人类相似度评级具有高度相关性，但缺乏可解释性。另一方面，基于图的含义表示（例如，抽象含义表示，AMR）的图度量可以明确两个句子相似的语义方面。然而，此类指标往往很慢，依赖于解析器，并且在评估句子相似性时无法达到最先进的性能。在这项工作中，我们的目标是两全其美，通过学习诱导 $S$emantically $S$tructured $S$entence BERT 嵌入 (S$^3$BERT)。我们的 S$^3$BERT 嵌入由可解释的子嵌入组成，强调各种语义句子特征（例如语义角色、否定或量化）。我们展示了如何 i) 通过近似一组可解释的 AMR 图指标来学习将句子嵌入分解为语义特征，以及如何 ii) 通过控制分解学习过程来保持神经嵌入的整体能力，第二个目标是强制与 SBERT 教师模型的相似性评级保持一致。在我们的实验研究中，我们表明我们的方法提供了可解释性，同时完全保留了神经句子嵌入的有效性和效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出S³BERT模型，将SBERT的句子嵌入分解为可解释的语义特征，结合神经网络嵌入的有效性与图基表示的可解释性。
AI.motivation: 现有SBERT等模型虽效果好但缺乏可解释性，而基于图的语义表示（如AMR）虽可解释但效率低且性能不足，需兼顾两者优势。
AI.method: 通过近似可解释的AMR图指标，将句子嵌入分解为语义子嵌入（如语义角色、否定、量化），并利用SBERT教师模型约束分解过程以保持相似性评分一致性。
AI.result: 实验表明，S³BERT在保持神经嵌入效率与效果的同时，提供了对语义特征的可解释性分解，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: S³BERT成功融合神经嵌入与图基表示的优势，为句子嵌入的可解释性研究提供了新方向。

conclusion: S³BERT成功融合神经嵌入与图基表示的优势，为句子嵌入的可解释性研究提供了新方向。

Our S$^3$BERT embeddings are composed of explainable sub-embeddings that emphasize various semantic sentence features (e.g., semantic roles, negation, or quantification). We show how to i) learn a decomposition of the sentence embeddings into semantic features, through approximation of a suite of interpretable AMR graph metrics, and how to ii) preserve the overall power of the neural embeddings by controlling the decomposition learning process with a second objective that enforces consistency with the similarity ratings of an SBERT teacher model. In our experimental studies, we show that our approach offers interpretability -- while fully preserving the effectiveness and efficiency of the neural sentence embeddings. 基于大型预训练语言模型的模型，例如 S(entence)BERT，提供了有效且高效的句子嵌入，与人类相似度评级具有高度相关性，但缺乏可解释性。另一方面，基于图的含义表示（例如，抽象含义表示，AMR）的图度量可以明确两个句子相似的语义方面。然而，此类指标往往很慢，依赖于解析器，并且在评估句子相似性时无法达到最先进的性能。在这项工作中，我们的目标是两全其美，通过学习诱导 $S$emantically $S$tructured $S$entence BERT 嵌入 (S$^3$BERT)。我们的 S$^3$BERT 嵌入由可解释的子嵌入组成，强调各种语义句子特征（例如语义角色、否定或量化）。我们展示了如何 i) 通过近似一组可解释的 AMR 图指标来学习将句子嵌入分解为语义特征，以及如何 ii) 通过控制分解学习过程来保持神经嵌入的整体能力，第二个目标是强制与 SBERT 教师模型的相似性评级保持一致。在我们的实验研究中，我们表明我们的方法提供了可解释性，同时完全保留了神经句子嵌入的有效性和效率。 本文提出S³BERT模型，将SBERT的句子嵌入分解为可解释的语义特征，结合神经网络嵌入的有效性与图基表示的可解释性。 Juri Opitz,Anette Frank cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Si Chen,Yi Zeng,Jiachen T. Wang,Won Park,Xun Chen,Lingjuan Lyu,Zhuoqing Mao,Ruoxi Jia
title: Turning a Curse into a Blessing: Enabling In-Distribution-Data-Free Backdoor Removal via Stabilized Model Inversion
title_zh: 化咒为福：通过稳定模型反演实现分布内无数据后门删除
comment: Because of an equation and author informational error, this paper has been withdrawn by the submitter
comments: Because of an equation and author informational error, this paper has been withdrawn by the submitter
abstract: Many backdoor removal techniques in machine learning models require clean in-distribution data, which may not always be available due to proprietary datasets. Model inversion techniques, often considered privacy threats, can reconstruct realistic training samples, potentially eliminating the need for in-distribution data. Prior attempts to combine backdoor removal and model inversion yielded limited results. Our work is the first to provide a thorough understanding of leveraging model inversion for effective backdoor removal by addressing key questions about reconstructed samples' properties, perceptual similarity, and the potential presence of backdoor triggers. We establish that relying solely on perceptual similarity is insufficient for robust defenses, and the stability of model predictions in response to input and parameter perturbations is also crucial. To tackle this, we introduce a novel bi-level optimization-based framework for model inversion, promoting stability and visual quality. Interestingly, we discover that reconstructed samples from a pre-trained generator's latent space are backdoor-free, even when utilizing signals from a backdoored model. We provide a theoretical analysis to support this finding. Our evaluation demonstrates that our stabilized model inversion technique achieves state-of-the-art backdoor removal performance without clean in-distribution data, matching or surpassing performance using the same amount of clean samples.
summary: Many backdoor removal techniques in machine learning models require clean in-distribution data, which may not always be available due to proprietary datasets. Model inversion techniques, often considered privacy threats, can reconstruct realistic training samples, potentially eliminating the need for in-distribution data. Prior attempts to combine backdoor removal and model inversion yielded limited results. Our work is the first to provide a thorough understanding of leveraging model inversion for effective backdoor removal by addressing key questions about reconstructed samples' properties, perceptual similarity, and the potential presence of backdoor triggers. We establish that relying solely on perceptual similarity is insufficient for robust defenses, and the stability of model predictions in response to input and parameter perturbations is also crucial. To tackle this, we introduce a novel bi-level optimization-based framework for model inversion, promoting stability and visual quality. Interestingly, we discover that reconstructed samples from a pre-trained generator's latent space are backdoor-free, even when utilizing signals from a backdoored model. We provide a theoretical analysis to support this finding. Our evaluation demonstrates that our stabilized model inversion technique achieves state-of-the-art backdoor removal performance without clean in-distribution data, matching or surpassing performance using the same amount of clean samples.
abstract_zh: 机器学习模型中的许多后门删除技术需要干净的分布内数据，但由于专有数据集，这些数据可能并不总是可用。模型反演技术通常被认为是隐私威胁，可以重建真实的训练样本，从而可能消除对分布数据的需求。之前将后门去除和模型反演结合起来的尝试取得了有限的结果。我们的工作首次通过解决有关重建样本的属性、感知相似性和后门触发器的潜在存在的关键问题，全面了解如何利用模型反演来有效去除后门。我们发现，仅仅依靠感知相似性不足以实现稳健的防御，模型预测响应输入和参数扰动的稳定性也至关重要。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了一种新颖的基于双层优化的模型反演框架，提高了稳定性和视觉质量。有趣的是，我们发现来自预训练生成器潜在空间的重建样本是无后门的，即使使用来自后门模型的信号也是如此。我们提供了理论分析来支持这一发现。我们的评估表明，我们的稳定模型反演技术在没有干净分布数据的情况下实现了最先进的后门去除性能，使用相同数量的干净样本匹配或超越性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需干净同分布数据的后门移除方法，通过稳定化模型逆向技术实现。
AI.motivation: 现有后门移除技术依赖难以获取的干净同分布数据，而模型逆向技术可能提供替代方案但此前效果有限。
AI.method: 设计基于双层优化的模型逆向框架，提升重建样本稳定性与视觉质量，并理论证明预训练生成器潜空间样本的无后门特性。
AI.result: 在无干净数据情况下实现最先进的后门移除效果，性能匹配或超越同等规模的干净样本方法。
AI.conclusion: 稳定化模型逆向技术可有效替代传统依赖干净数据的后门移除方案，为隐私保护提供新思路。

authors_text: Quanzeng You,Jiang Wang,Peng Chu,Andre Abrantes,Zicheng Liu
title: Consistent Video Instance Segmentation with Inter-Frame Recurrent Attention
title_zh: 具有帧间循环注意力的一致视频实例分割
abstract: Video instance segmentation aims at predicting object segmentation masks for each frame, as well as associating the instances across multiple frames. Recent end-to-end video instance segmentation methods are capable of performing object segmentation and instance association together in a direct parallel sequence decoding/prediction framework. Although these methods generally predict higher quality object segmentation masks, they can fail to associate instances in challenging cases because they do not explicitly model the temporal instance consistency for adjacent frames. We propose a consistent end-to-end video instance segmentation framework with Inter-Frame Recurrent Attention to model both the temporal instance consistency for adjacent frames and the global temporal context. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that the Inter-Frame Recurrent Attention significantly improves temporal instance consistency while maintaining the quality of the object segmentation masks. Our model achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on both YouTubeVIS-2019 (62.1\%) and YouTubeVIS-2021 (54.7\%) datasets. In addition, quantitative and qualitative results show that the proposed methods predict more temporally consistent instance segmentation masks.
summary: Video instance segmentation aims at predicting object segmentation masks for each frame, as well as associating the instances across multiple frames. Recent end-to-end video instance segmentation methods are capable of performing object segmentation and instance association together in a direct parallel sequence decoding/prediction framework. Although these methods generally predict higher quality object segmentation masks, they can fail to associate instances in challenging cases because they do not explicitly model the temporal instance consistency for adjacent frames. We propose a consistent end-to-end video instance segmentation framework with Inter-Frame Recurrent Attention to model both the temporal instance consistency for adjacent frames and the global temporal context. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that the Inter-Frame Recurrent Attention significantly improves temporal instance consistency while maintaining the quality of the object segmentation masks. Our model achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on both YouTubeVIS-2019 (62.1\%) and YouTubeVIS-2021 (54.7\%) datasets. In addition, quantitative and qualitative results show that the proposed methods predict more temporally consistent instance segmentation masks.
abstract_zh: 视频实例分割旨在预测每个帧的对象分割掩模，以及关联多个帧的实例。最近的端到端视频实例分割方法能够在直接并行序列解码/预测框架中一起执行对象分割和实例关联。尽管这些方法通常预测更高质量的对象分割掩模，但它们可能无法在具有挑战性的情况下关联实例，因为它们没有显式地建模相邻帧的时间实例一致性。我们提出了一种具有帧间循环注意力的一致的端到端视频实例分割框架，以对相邻帧的时间实例一致性和全局时间上下文进行建模。我们的大量实验表明，帧间循环注意力显着提高了时间实例的一致性，同时保持了对象分割掩模的质量。我们的模型在 YouTubeVIS-2019 (62.1\%) 和 YouTubeVIS-2021 (54.7\%) 数据集上均达到了最先进的准确性。此外，定量和定性结果表明，所提出的方法可以预测时间上更加一致的实例分割掩模。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于帧间循环注意力的端到端视频实例分割框架，以提升相邻帧之间实例分割的一致性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法虽能生成高质量分割掩码，但因未显式建模相邻帧的时序一致性，导致复杂场景下实例关联失效。
AI.method: 设计包含帧间循环注意力机制的框架，同时建模相邻帧的时序一致性与全局时序上下文，实现端到端的分割与关联。
AI.result: 在YouTubeVIS-2019和2021数据集上分别达到62.1%和54.7%的准确率，显著提升时序一致性且保持分割质量。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过显式建模时序一致性，在保持分割精度的同时实现更优的实例关联，验证了框架的有效性。

authors_text: Matt Deitke,Eli VanderBilt,Alvaro Herrasti,Luca Weihs,Jordi Salvador,Kiana Ehsani,Winson Han,Eric Kolve,Ali Farhadi,Aniruddha Kembhavi,Roozbeh Mottaghi
title: ProcTHOR: Large-Scale Embodied AI Using Procedural Generation
title_zh: ProcTHOR：使用程序生成的大规模嵌入式人工智能
abstract: Massive datasets and high-capacity models have driven many recent advancements in computer vision and natural language understanding. This work presents a platform to enable similar success stories in Embodied AI. We propose ProcTHOR, a framework for procedural generation of Embodied AI environments. ProcTHOR enables us to sample arbitrarily large datasets of diverse, interactive, customizable, and performant virtual environments to train and evaluate embodied agents across navigation, interaction, and manipulation tasks. We demonstrate the power and potential of ProcTHOR via a sample of 10,000 generated houses and a simple neural model. Models trained using only RGB images on ProcTHOR, with no explicit mapping and no human task supervision produce state-of-the-art results across 6 embodied AI benchmarks for navigation, rearrangement, and arm manipulation, including the presently running Habitat 2022, AI2-THOR Rearrangement 2022, and RoboTHOR challenges. We also demonstrate strong 0-shot results on these benchmarks, via pre-training on ProcTHOR with no fine-tuning on the downstream benchmark, often beating previous state-of-the-art systems that access the downstream training data.
summary: Massive datasets and high-capacity models have driven many recent advancements in computer vision and natural language understanding. This work presents a platform to enable similar success stories in Embodied AI. We propose ProcTHOR, a framework for procedural generation of Embodied AI environments. ProcTHOR enables us to sample arbitrarily large datasets of diverse, interactive, customizable, and performant virtual environments to train and evaluate embodied agents across navigation, interaction, and manipulation tasks. We demonstrate the power and potential of ProcTHOR via a sample of 10,000 generated houses and a simple neural model. Models trained using only RGB images on ProcTHOR, with no explicit mapping and no human task supervision produce state-of-the-art results across 6 embodied AI benchmarks for navigation, rearrangement, and arm manipulation, including the presently running Habitat 2022, AI2-THOR Rearrangement 2022, and RoboTHOR challenges. We also demonstrate strong 0-shot results on these benchmarks, via pre-training on ProcTHOR with no fine-tuning on the downstream benchmark, often beating previous state-of-the-art systems that access the downstream training data.
abstract_zh: 海量数据集和高容量模型推动了计算机视觉和自然语言理解领域的许多最新进展。这项工作提供了一个平台，可以在嵌入式人工智能中实现类似的成功故事。我们提出了 ProcTHOR，一个用于程序化生成人工智能环境的框架。 ProcTHOR 使我们能够对多样化、交互式、可定制和高性能虚拟环境的任意大型数据集进行采样，以跨导航、交互和操作任务训练和评估实体代理。我们通过 10,000 个生成的房屋样本和一个简单的神经模型展示了 ProcTHOR 的力量和潜力。仅使用 ProcTHOR 上的 RGB 图像训练的模型，没有显式映射，也没有人工任务监督，在导航、重新排列和手臂操作的 6 个具体 AI 基准上产生了最先进的结果，包括目前运行的 Habitat 2022、AI2-THOR Rearrangement 2022 和 RoboTHOR 挑战。我们还在这些基准上展示了强大的 0-shot 结果，通过 ProcTHOR 上的预训练，无需对下游基准进行微调，通常击败了访问下游训练数据的先前最先进的系统。

AI.motivation: 当前具身AI缺乏大规模、多样化且可定制的训练环境，限制了模型性能提升。
AI.method: 基于程序化生成技术构建ProcTHOR平台，生成包含10,000栋房屋的多样化虚拟环境，并利用RGB图像训练神经网络模型。
AI.result: 在6个具身AI基准测试中取得SOTA结果，且无需微调即可实现零样本迁移，超越依赖下游数据的现有系统。
AI.conclusion: ProcTHOR为具身AI提供了高效且可扩展的训练平台，证明了程序化生成环境在提升代理导航、交互和操作能力方面的有效性。

search_text: ProcTHOR: Large-Scale Embodied AI Using Procedural Generation ProcTHOR：使用程序生成的大规模嵌入式人工智能 Massive datasets and high-capacity models have driven many recent advancements in computer vision and natural language understanding. This work presents a platform to enable similar success stories in Embodied AI. We propose ProcTHOR, a framework for procedural generation of Embodied AI environments. ProcTHOR enables us to sample arbitrarily large datasets of diverse, interactive, customizable, and performant virtual environments to train and evaluate embodied agents across navigation, interaction, and manipulation tasks. We demonstrate the power and potential of ProcTHOR via a sample of 10,000 generated houses and a simple neural model. Models trained using only RGB images on ProcTHOR, with no explicit mapping and no human task supervision produce state-of-the-art results across 6 embodied AI benchmarks for navigation, rearrangement, and arm manipulation, including the presently running Habitat 2022, AI2-THOR Rearrangement 2022, and RoboTHOR challenges. We also demonstrate strong 0-shot results on these benchmarks, via pre-training on ProcTHOR with no fine-tuning on the downstream benchmark, often beating previous state-of-the-art systems that access the downstream training data. 海量数据集和高容量模型推动了计算机视觉和自然语言理解领域的许多最新进展。这项工作提供了一个平台，可以在嵌入式人工智能中实现类似的成功故事。我们提出了 ProcTHOR，一个用于程序化生成人工智能环境的框架。 ProcTHOR 使我们能够对多样化、交互式、可定制和高性能虚拟环境的任意大型数据集进行采样，以跨导航、交互和操作任务训练和评估实体代理。我们通过 10,000 个生成的房屋样本和一个简单的神经模型展示了 ProcTHOR 的力量和潜力。仅使用 ProcTHOR 上的 RGB 图像训练的模型，没有显式映射，也没有人工任务监督，在导航、重新排列和手臂操作的 6 个具体 AI 基准上产生了最先进的结果，包括目前运行的 Habitat 2022、AI2-THOR Rearrangement 2022 和 RoboTHOR 挑战。我们还在这些基准上展示了强大的 0-shot 结果，通过 ProcTHOR 上的预训练，无需对下游基准进行微调，通常击败了访问下游训练数据的先前最先进的系统。 本文提出ProcTHOR框架，通过程序化生成大规模虚拟环境以推动具身人工智能的发展。 Matt Deitke,Eli VanderBilt,Alvaro Herrasti,Luca Weihs,Jordi Salvador,Kiana Ehsani,Winson Han,Eric Kolve,Ali Farhadi,Aniruddha Kembhavi,Roozbeh Mottaghi cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.RO

authors_text: Niclas Boehmer,Tomohiro Koana
title: The Complexity of Finding Fair Many-to-One Matchings
title_zh: 寻找公平的多对一匹配的复杂性
abstract: We analyze the (parameterized) computational complexity of "fair" variants of bipartite many-to-one matching, where each vertex from the "left" side is matched to exactly one vertex and each vertex from the "right" side may be matched to multiple vertices. We want to find a "fair" matching, in which each vertex from the right side is matched to a "fair" set of vertices. Assuming that each vertex from the left side has one color modeling its attribute, we study two fairness criteria. In one of them, we deem a vertex set fair if for any two colors, the difference between the numbers of their occurrences does not exceed a given threshold. Fairness is relevant when finding many-to-one matchings between students and colleges, voters and constituencies, and applicants and firms. Here colors may model sociodemographic attributes, party memberships, and qualifications, respectively. We show that finding a fair many-to-one matching is NP-hard even for three colors and maximum degree five. Our main contribution is the design of fixed-parameter tractable algorithms with respect to the number of vertices on the right side. Our algorithms make use of a variety of techniques including color coding. At the core lie integer linear programs encoding Hall like conditions. To establish the correctness of our integer programs, we prove a new separation result, inspired by Frank's separation theorem [Frank, Discrete Math. 1982], which may also be of independent interest. We further obtain complete complexity dichotomies regarding the number of colors and the maximum degree of each side.
summary: We analyze the (parameterized) computational complexity of "fair" variants of bipartite many-to-one matching, where each vertex from the "left" side is matched to exactly one vertex and each vertex from the "right" side may be matched to multiple vertices. We want to find a "fair" matching, in which each vertex from the right side is matched to a "fair" set of vertices. Assuming that each vertex from the left side has one color modeling its attribute, we study two fairness criteria. In one of them, we deem a vertex set fair if for any two colors, the difference between the numbers of their occurrences does not exceed a given threshold. Fairness is relevant when finding many-to-one matchings between students and colleges, voters and constituencies, and applicants and firms. Here colors may model sociodemographic attributes, party memberships, and qualifications, respectively. We show that finding a fair many-to-one matching is NP-hard even for three colors and maximum degree five. Our main contribution is the design of fixed-parameter tractable algorithms with respect to the number of vertices on the right side. Our algorithms make use of a variety of techniques including color coding. At the core lie integer linear programs encoding Hall like conditions. To establish the correctness of our integer programs, we prove a new separation result, inspired by Frank's separation theorem [Frank, Discrete Math. 1982], which may also be of independent interest. We further obtain complete complexity dichotomies regarding the number of colors and the maximum degree of each side.
abstract_zh: 我们分析了二分多对一匹配的“公平”变体的（参数化）计算复杂度，其中“左”侧的每个顶点恰好匹配一个顶点，而“右”侧的每个顶点可以匹配多个顶点。我们想要找到一个“公平”的匹配，其中右侧的每个顶点都与一组“公平”的顶点匹配。假设左侧的每个顶点都有一种颜色来建模其属性，我们研究两个公平标准。在其中之一中，如果对于任何两种颜色，它们出现的次数之间的差异不超过给定的阈值，我们认为顶点集是公平的。在寻找学生和大学、选民和选区、申请人和公司之间的多对一匹配时，公平性至关重要。这里，颜色可以分别模拟社会人口统计属性、党员身份和资格。我们证明，即使对于三种颜色和最大程度为五，找到公平的多对一匹配也是 NP 困难的。我们的主要贡献是设计关于右侧顶点数量的固定参数易处理算法。我们的算法利用了多种技术，包括颜色编码。其核心在于编码类似霍尔条件的整数线性程序。为了确定整数程序的正确性，我们受弗兰克分离定理的启发，证明了一个新的分离结果[Frank，离散数学。 1982]，这也可能具有独立利益。我们进一步获得关于颜色数量和每条边的最大程度的完整复杂性二分法。

AI.tldr: 本文分析了带公平性约束的二分图多对一匹配问题的计算复杂性，并设计了固定参数可解算法。
AI.motivation: 公平匹配在学生与院校、选民与选区等实际场景中具有重要意义，需平衡不同属性群体的分配。
AI.method: 采用参数化复杂性分析、颜色编码技术及整数线性规划，结合Frank分离定理证明新分离结果。
AI.result: 证明三色且最大度五时问题NP难，设计基于右侧顶点数的固定参数算法，并建立颜色数与度数的复杂性二分法。
AI.conclusion: 提出高效算法框架并揭示问题复杂性边界，为公平匹配实践提供理论支持与计算工具。

authors_text: Chencheng Liang,Philipp Rümmer,Marc Brockschmidt
title: Exploring Representation of Horn Clauses using GNNs (Extended Technical Report)
title_zh: 使用 GNN 探索 Horn 子句的表示（扩展技术报告）
abstract: Learning program semantics from raw source code is challenging due to the complexity of real-world programming language syntax and due to the difficulty of reconstructing long-distance relational information implicitly represented in programs using identifiers. Addressing the first point, we consider Constrained Horn Clauses (CHCs) as a standard representation of program verification problems, providing a simple and programming language-independent syntax. For the second challenge, we explore graph representations of CHCs, and propose a new Relational Hypergraph Neural Network (R-HyGNN) architecture to learn program features. We introduce two different graph representations of CHCs. One is called constraint graph (CG), and emphasizes syntactic information of CHCs by translating the symbols and their relations in CHCs as typed nodes and binary edges, respectively, and constructing the constraints as abstract syntax trees. The second one is called control- and data-flow hypergraph (CDHG), and emphasizes semantic information of CHCs by representing the control and data flow through ternary hyperedges. We then propose a new GNN architecture, R-HyGNN, extending Relational Graph Convolutional Networks, to handle hypergraphs. To evaluate the ability of R-HyGNN to extract semantic information from programs, we use R-HyGNNs to train models on the two graph representations, and on five proxy tasks with increasing difficulty, using benchmarks from CHC-COMP 2021 as training data. The most difficult proxy task requires the model to predict the occurrence of clauses in counter-examples, which subsumes satisfiability of CHCs. CDHG achieves 90.59% accuracy in this task. Furthermore, R-HyGNN has perfect predictions on one of the graphs consisting of more than 290 clauses. Overall, our experiments indicate that R-HyGNN can capture intricate program features for guiding verification problems.
summary: Learning program semantics from raw source code is challenging due to the complexity of real-world programming language syntax and due to the difficulty of reconstructing long-distance relational information implicitly represented in programs using identifiers. Addressing the first point, we consider Constrained Horn Clauses (CHCs) as a standard representation of program verification problems, providing a simple and programming language-independent syntax. For the second challenge, we explore graph representations of CHCs, and propose a new Relational Hypergraph Neural Network (R-HyGNN) architecture to learn program features. We introduce two different graph representations of CHCs. One is called constraint graph (CG), and emphasizes syntactic information of CHCs by translating the symbols and their relations in CHCs as typed nodes and binary edges, respectively, and constructing the constraints as abstract syntax trees. The second one is called control- and data-flow hypergraph (CDHG), and emphasizes semantic information of CHCs by representing the control and data flow through ternary hyperedges. We then propose a new GNN architecture, R-HyGNN, extending Relational Graph Convolutional Networks, to handle hypergraphs. To evaluate the ability of R-HyGNN to extract semantic information from programs, we use R-HyGNNs to train models on the two graph representations, and on five proxy tasks with increasing difficulty, using benchmarks from CHC-COMP 2021 as training data. The most difficult proxy task requires the model to predict the occurrence of clauses in counter-examples, which subsumes satisfiability of CHCs. CDHG achieves 90.59% accuracy in this task. Furthermore, R-HyGNN has perfect predictions on one of the graphs consisting of more than 290 clauses. Overall, our experiments indicate that R-HyGNN can capture intricate program features for guiding verification problems.
abstract_zh: 由于现实世界编程语言语法的复杂性以及使用标识符重建程序中隐式表示的长距离关系信息的困难，从原始源代码中学习程序语义具有挑战性。针对第一点，我们将约束霍恩子句（CHC）视为程序验证问题的标准表示，提供简单且独立于编程语言的语法。对于第二个挑战，我们探索 CHC 的图表示，并提出一种新的关系超图神经网络 (R-HyGNN) 架构来学习程序特征。我们引入两种不同的 CHC 图形表示。一种称为约束图（CG），通过将CHC中的符号及其关系分别转换为类型化节点和二元边，并将约束构造为抽象语法树来强调CHC的句法信息。第二个称为控制和数据流超图（CDHG），通过三元超边表示控制和数据流来强调 CHC 的语义信息。然后，我们提出了一种新的 GNN 架构 R-HyGNN，扩展关系图卷积网络来处理超图。为了评估 R-HyGNN 从程序中提取语义信息的能力，我们使用 CHC-COMP 2021 的基准作为训练数据，使用 R-HyGNN 在两个图表示以及五个难度逐渐增加的代理任务上训练模型。最困难的代理任务要求模型预测反例中子句的出现，其中包含 CHC 的可满足性。 CDHG 在这项任务中达到了 90.59% 的准确率。此外，R-HyGNN 对包含 290 多个子句的图之一具有完美的预测。总的来说，我们的实验表明 R-HyGNN 可以捕获复杂的程序特征来指导验证问题。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于图神经网络（R-HyGNN）的新方法，用于从约束霍恩子句（CHCs）中学习程序语义，通过两种图表示（约束图和控制-数据流超图）解决程序验证中的复杂语法和长距离关系问题。
AI.motivation: 程序验证中需处理复杂语法和隐式长距离关系，传统方法难以有效建模。CHCs提供了一种简洁的语法表示，但需更有效的图结构来捕捉语义信息。
AI.method: 设计两种CHC图表示：约束图（CG）和控制-数据流超图（CDHG），并扩展关系图卷积网络为R-HyGNN，以处理超图结构，通过五个渐进式任务评估模型性能。
AI.result: CDHG在最难任务中达到90.59%准确率，且在包含290多条子句的图上实现完美预测，验证了R-HyGNN捕捉程序特征的能力。
AI.conclusion: R-HyGNN能有效提取程序语义特征，为程序验证问题提供新的解决方案，尤其在处理复杂控制流和数据流关系时表现优异。

search_text: Exploring Representation of Horn Clauses using GNNs (Extended Technical Report) 使用 GNN 探索 Horn 子句的表示（扩展技术报告） Learning program semantics from raw source code is challenging due to the complexity of real-world programming language syntax and due to the difficulty of reconstructing long-distance relational information implicitly represented in programs using identifiers. Addressing the first point, we consider Constrained Horn Clauses (CHCs) as a standard representation of program verification problems, providing a simple and programming language-independent syntax. For the second challenge, we explore graph representations of CHCs, and propose a new Relational Hypergraph Neural Network (R-HyGNN) architecture to learn program features. We introduce two different graph representations of CHCs. One is called constraint graph (CG), and emphasizes syntactic information of CHCs by translating the symbols and their relations in CHCs as typed nodes and binary edges, respectively, and constructing the constraints as abstract syntax trees. The second one is called control- and data-flow hypergraph (CDHG), and emphasizes semantic information of CHCs by representing the control and data flow through ternary hyperedges. We then propose a new GNN architecture, R-HyGNN, extending Relational Graph Convolutional Networks, to handle hypergraphs. To evaluate the ability of R-HyGNN to extract semantic information from programs, we use R-HyGNNs to train models on the two graph representations, and on five proxy tasks with increasing difficulty, using benchmarks from CHC-COMP 2021 as training data. The most difficult proxy task requires the model to predict the occurrence of clauses in counter-examples, which subsumes satisfiability of CHCs. CDHG achieves 90.59% accuracy in this task. Furthermore, R-HyGNN has perfect predictions on one of the graphs consisting of more than 290 clauses. Overall, our experiments indicate that R-HyGNN can capture intricate program features for guiding verification problems. 由于现实世界编程语言语法的复杂性以及使用标识符重建程序中隐式表示的长距离关系信息的困难，从原始源代码中学习程序语义具有挑战性。针对第一点，我们将约束霍恩子句（CHC）视为程序验证问题的标准表示，提供简单且独立于编程语言的语法。对于第二个挑战，我们探索 CHC 的图表示，并提出一种新的关系超图神经网络 (R-HyGNN) 架构来学习程序特征。我们引入两种不同的 CHC 图形表示。一种称为约束图（CG），通过将CHC中的符号及其关系分别转换为类型化节点和二元边，并将约束构造为抽象语法树来强调CHC的句法信息。第二个称为控制和数据流超图（CDHG），通过三元超边表示控制和数据流来强调 CHC 的语义信息。然后，我们提出了一种新的 GNN 架构 R-HyGNN，扩展关系图卷积网络来处理超图。为了评估 R-HyGNN 从程序中提取语义信息的能力，我们使用 CHC-COMP 2021 的基准作为训练数据，使用 R-HyGNN 在两个图表示以及五个难度逐渐增加的代理任务上训练模型。最困难的代理任务要求模型预测反例中子句的出现，其中包含 CHC 的可满足性。 CDHG 在这项任务中达到了 90.59% 的准确率。此外，R-HyGNN 对包含 290 多个子句的图之一具有完美的预测。总的来说，我们的实验表明 R-HyGNN 可以捕获复杂的程序特征来指导验证问题。 本文提出了一种基于图神经网络（R-HyGNN）的新方法，用于从约束霍恩子句（CHCs）中学习程序语义，通过两种图表示（约束图和控制-数据流超图）解决程序验证中的复杂语法和长距离关系问题。 Chencheng Liang,Philipp Rümmer,Marc Brockschmidt cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Xuan-Tung Nguyen,Minh-Duong Nguyen,Quoc-Viet Pham,Vinh-Quang Do,Won-Joo Hwang
title: Resource Allocation for Compression-aided Federated Learning with High Distortion Rate
title_zh: 高失真率压缩辅助联邦学习的资源分配
abstract: Recently, a considerable amount of works have been made to tackle the communication burden in federated learning (FL) (e.g., model quantization, data sparsification, and model compression). However, the existing methods, that boost the communication efficiency in FL, result in a considerable trade-off between communication efficiency and global convergence rate. We formulate an optimization problem for compression-aided FL, which captures the relationship between the distortion rate, number of participating IoT devices, and convergence rate. Following that, the objective function is to minimize the total transmission time for FL convergence. Because the problem is non-convex, we propose to decompose it into sub-problems. Based on the property of a FL model, we first determine the number of IoT devices participating in the FL process. Then, the communication between IoT devices and the server is optimized by efficiently allocating wireless resources based on a coalition game. Our theoretical analysis shows that, by actively controlling the number of participating IoT devices, we can avoid the training divergence of compression-aided FL while maintaining the communication efficiency.
summary: Recently, a considerable amount of works have been made to tackle the communication burden in federated learning (FL) (e.g., model quantization, data sparsification, and model compression). However, the existing methods, that boost the communication efficiency in FL, result in a considerable trade-off between communication efficiency and global convergence rate. We formulate an optimization problem for compression-aided FL, which captures the relationship between the distortion rate, number of participating IoT devices, and convergence rate. Following that, the objective function is to minimize the total transmission time for FL convergence. Because the problem is non-convex, we propose to decompose it into sub-problems. Based on the property of a FL model, we first determine the number of IoT devices participating in the FL process. Then, the communication between IoT devices and the server is optimized by efficiently allocating wireless resources based on a coalition game. Our theoretical analysis shows that, by actively controlling the number of participating IoT devices, we can avoid the training divergence of compression-aided FL while maintaining the communication efficiency.
abstract_zh: 最近，为了解决联邦学习（FL）中的通信负担（例如模型量化、数据稀疏化和模型压缩），人们进行了大量的工作。然而，现有的提高 FL 通信效率的方法导致通信效率和全局收敛速度之间存在相当大的权衡。我们制定了压缩辅助 FL 的优化问题，它捕获了失真率、参与的物联网设备数量和收敛速度之间的关系。接下来，目标函数是最小化 FL 收敛的总传输时间。由于问题是非凸的，我们建议将其分解为子问题。根据 FL 模型的属性，我们首先确定参与 FL 过程的 IoT 设备的数量。然后，通过基于联盟博弈有效分配无线资源来优化物联网设备和服务器之间的通信。我们的理论分析表明，通过主动控制参与的物联网设备数量，我们可以在保持通信效率的同时避免压缩辅助 FL 的训练发散。

AI.tldr: 本文研究压缩辅助联邦学习中的资源分配问题，旨在平衡通信效率与全局收敛率之间的矛盾。
AI.motivation: 现有联邦学习通信优化方法（如模型压缩）会导致通信效率与收敛速度的显著权衡，亟需解决这一矛盾。
AI.method: 建立非凸优化模型，分解为设备数量确定与无线资源分配两个子问题，结合联盟博弈理论进行资源优化。
AI.result: 理论分析证明通过动态控制参与设备数量可避免训练发散，同时维持通信效率。
AI.conclusion: 主动调控参与设备规模是解决压缩辅助联邦学习中通信-收敛权衡问题的有效途径。

authors_text: Zhengyuan Shi,Min Li,Sadaf Khan,Liuzheng Wang,Naixing Wang,Yu Huang,Qiang Xu
title: DeepTPI: Test Point Insertion with Deep Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: DeepTPI：通过深度强化学习插入测试点
abstract: Test point insertion (TPI) is a widely used technique for testability enhancement, especially for logic built-in self-test (LBIST) due to its relatively low fault coverage. In this paper, we propose a novel TPI approach based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL), named DeepTPI. Unlike previous learning-based solutions that formulate the TPI task as a supervised-learning problem, we train a novel DRL agent, instantiated as the combination of a graph neural network (GNN) and a Deep Q-Learning network (DQN), to maximize the test coverage improvement. Specifically, we model circuits as directed graphs and design a graph-based value network to estimate the action values for inserting different test points. The policy of the DRL agent is defined as selecting the action with the maximum value. Moreover, we apply the general node embeddings from a pre-trained model to enhance node features, and propose a dedicated testability-aware attention mechanism for the value network. Experimental results on circuits with various scales show that DeepTPI significantly improves test coverage compared to the commercial DFT tool. The code of this work is available at https://github.com/cure-lab/DeepTPI.
summary: Test point insertion (TPI) is a widely used technique for testability enhancement, especially for logic built-in self-test (LBIST) due to its relatively low fault coverage. In this paper, we propose a novel TPI approach based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL), named DeepTPI. Unlike previous learning-based solutions that formulate the TPI task as a supervised-learning problem, we train a novel DRL agent, instantiated as the combination of a graph neural network (GNN) and a Deep Q-Learning network (DQN), to maximize the test coverage improvement. Specifically, we model circuits as directed graphs and design a graph-based value network to estimate the action values for inserting different test points. The policy of the DRL agent is defined as selecting the action with the maximum value. Moreover, we apply the general node embeddings from a pre-trained model to enhance node features, and propose a dedicated testability-aware attention mechanism for the value network. Experimental results on circuits with various scales show that DeepTPI significantly improves test coverage compared to the commercial DFT tool. The code of this work is available at https://github.com/cure-lab/DeepTPI.
abstract_zh: 测试点插入 (TPI) 是一种广泛使用的可测试性增强技术，尤其是逻辑内置自测试 (LBIST)，因为其故障覆盖率相对较低。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于深度强化学习（DRL）的新型 TPI 方法，称为 DeepTPI。与之前将 TPI 任务表述为监督学习问题的基于学习的解决方案不同，我们训练了一种新颖的 DRL 代理，将其实例化为图神经网络 (GNN) 和深度 Q 学习网络 (DQN) 的组合，以最大限度地提高测试覆盖率。具体来说，我们将电路建模为有向图，并设计基于图的价值网络来估计插入不同测试点的动作值。 DRL代理的策略被定义为选择具有最大值的动作。此外，我们应用预训练模型中的通用节点嵌入来增强节点特征，并为价值网络提出了一种专用的可测试性注意机制。在不同规模电路上的实验结果表明，与商业DFT工具相比，DeepTPI显着提高了测试覆盖率。这项工作的代码可在 https://github.com/cure-lab/DeepTPI 获取。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于深度强化学习的测试点插入方法DeepTPI，通过图神经网络与深度Q学习网络结合，提升测试覆盖率。
AI.motivation: 传统测试点插入方法在逻辑内置自测试（LBIST）中故障覆盖率不足，需更智能的优化策略以提高测试效率。
AI.method: 将电路建模为有向图，设计图基值网络估计动作值，结合预训练节点嵌入与可测试性感知注意力机制，采用DRL代理选择最优测试点插入策略。
AI.result: 在多规模电路实验中，DeepTPI显著优于商业DFT工具，有效提升测试覆盖率。
AI.conclusion: DeepTPI通过强化学习框架与图神经网络的结合，验证了其在测试点插入任务中的有效性与优越性。

ai_content.tldr: 提出一种基于深度强化学习的测试点插入方法DeepTPI，通过图神经网络与深度Q学习网络结合，提升测试覆盖率。

ai_content.result: 在多规模电路实验中，DeepTPI显著优于商业DFT工具，有效提升测试覆盖率。

search_text: DeepTPI: Test Point Insertion with Deep Reinforcement Learning DeepTPI：通过深度强化学习插入测试点 Test point insertion (TPI) is a widely used technique for testability enhancement, especially for logic built-in self-test (LBIST) due to its relatively low fault coverage. In this paper, we propose a novel TPI approach based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL), named DeepTPI. Unlike previous learning-based solutions that formulate the TPI task as a supervised-learning problem, we train a novel DRL agent, instantiated as the combination of a graph neural network (GNN) and a Deep Q-Learning network (DQN), to maximize the test coverage improvement. Specifically, we model circuits as directed graphs and design a graph-based value network to estimate the action values for inserting different test points. The policy of the DRL agent is defined as selecting the action with the maximum value. Moreover, we apply the general node embeddings from a pre-trained model to enhance node features, and propose a dedicated testability-aware attention mechanism for the value network. Experimental results on circuits with various scales show that DeepTPI significantly improves test coverage compared to the commercial DFT tool. The code of this work is available at https://github.com/cure-lab/DeepTPI. 测试点插入 (TPI) 是一种广泛使用的可测试性增强技术，尤其是逻辑内置自测试 (LBIST)，因为其故障覆盖率相对较低。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于深度强化学习（DRL）的新型 TPI 方法，称为 DeepTPI。与之前将 TPI 任务表述为监督学习问题的基于学习的解决方案不同，我们训练了一种新颖的 DRL 代理，将其实例化为图神经网络 (GNN) 和深度 Q 学习网络 (DQN) 的组合，以最大限度地提高测试覆盖率。具体来说，我们将电路建模为有向图，并设计基于图的价值网络来估计插入不同测试点的动作值。 DRL代理的策略被定义为选择具有最大值的动作。此外，我们应用预训练模型中的通用节点嵌入来增强节点特征，并为价值网络提出了一种专用的可测试性注意机制。在不同规模电路上的实验结果表明，与商业DFT工具相比，DeepTPI显着提高了测试覆盖率。这项工作的代码可在 https://github.com/cure-lab/DeepTPI 获取。 提出一种基于深度强化学习的测试点插入方法DeepTPI，通过图神经网络与深度Q学习网络结合，提升测试覆盖率。 Zhengyuan Shi,Min Li,Sadaf Khan,Liuzheng Wang,Naixing Wang,Yu Huang,Qiang Xu cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.AR

authors_text: Tianyu Zhang,Amin Banitalebi-Dehkordi,Yong Zhang
title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Exact Combinatorial Optimization: Learning to Branch
title_zh: 用于精确组合优化的深度强化学习：学习分支
abstract: Branch-and-bound is a systematic enumerative method for combinatorial optimization, where the performance highly relies on the variable selection strategy. State-of-the-art handcrafted heuristic strategies suffer from relatively slow inference time for each selection, while the current machine learning methods require a significant amount of labeled data. We propose a new approach for solving the data labeling and inference latency issues in combinatorial optimization based on the use of the reinforcement learning (RL) paradigm. We use imitation learning to bootstrap an RL agent and then use Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to further explore global optimal actions. Then, a value network is used to run Monte-Carlo tree search (MCTS) to enhance the policy network. We evaluate the performance of our method on four different categories of combinatorial optimization problems and show that our approach performs strongly compared to the state-of-the-art machine learning and heuristics based methods.
summary: Branch-and-bound is a systematic enumerative method for combinatorial optimization, where the performance highly relies on the variable selection strategy. State-of-the-art handcrafted heuristic strategies suffer from relatively slow inference time for each selection, while the current machine learning methods require a significant amount of labeled data. We propose a new approach for solving the data labeling and inference latency issues in combinatorial optimization based on the use of the reinforcement learning (RL) paradigm. We use imitation learning to bootstrap an RL agent and then use Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to further explore global optimal actions. Then, a value network is used to run Monte-Carlo tree search (MCTS) to enhance the policy network. We evaluate the performance of our method on four different categories of combinatorial optimization problems and show that our approach performs strongly compared to the state-of-the-art machine learning and heuristics based methods.
abstract_zh: 分支定界是一种用于组合优化的系统枚举方法，其性能高度依赖于变量选择策略。最先进的手工启发式策略的每个选择的推理时间相对较慢，而当前的机器学习方法需要大量的标记数据。我们提出了一种基于强化学习（RL）范式的组合优化中解决数据标记和推理延迟问题的新方法。我们使用模仿学习来引导 RL 代理，然后使用近端策略优化 (PPO) 来进一步探索全局最优操作。然后，使用价值网络来运行蒙特卡罗树搜索（MCTS）以增强策略网络。我们评估了我们的方法在四种不同类别的组合优化问题上的性能，并表明我们的方法与最先进的机器学习和启发式方法相比表现强劲。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于深度强化学习的分支策略，以提升组合优化问题的求解效率。
AI.motivation: 传统启发式分支策略推理速度慢，机器学习方法依赖大量标注数据，需解决数据标注与推理延迟问题。
AI.method: 结合模仿学习初始化RL智能体，采用PPO优化策略，并通过价值网络与MCTS增强策略网络。
AI.result: 在四类组合优化问题上表现优于现有机器学习与启发式方法。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法有效融合强化学习与树搜索，为组合优化提供高效求解方案。

authors_text: Amin Banitalebi-Dehkordi,Pratik Gujjar,Yong Zhang
title: AuxMix: Semi-Supervised Learning with Unconstrained Unlabeled Data
title_zh: AuxMix：使用无约束无标签数据的半监督学习
abstract: Semi-supervised learning (SSL) has seen great strides when labeled data is scarce but unlabeled data is abundant. Critically, most recent work assume that such unlabeled data is drawn from the same distribution as the labeled data. In this work, we show that state-of-the-art SSL algorithms suffer a degradation in performance in the presence of unlabeled auxiliary data that does not necessarily possess the same class distribution as the labeled set. We term this problem as Auxiliary-SSL and propose AuxMix, an algorithm that leverages self-supervised learning tasks to learn generic features in order to mask auxiliary data that are not semantically similar to the labeled set. We also propose to regularize learning by maximizing the predicted entropy for dissimilar auxiliary samples. We show an improvement of 5% over existing baselines on a ResNet-50 model when trained on CIFAR10 dataset with 4k labeled samples and all unlabeled data is drawn from the Tiny-ImageNet dataset. We report competitive results on several datasets and conduct ablation studies.
summary: Semi-supervised learning (SSL) has seen great strides when labeled data is scarce but unlabeled data is abundant. Critically, most recent work assume that such unlabeled data is drawn from the same distribution as the labeled data. In this work, we show that state-of-the-art SSL algorithms suffer a degradation in performance in the presence of unlabeled auxiliary data that does not necessarily possess the same class distribution as the labeled set. We term this problem as Auxiliary-SSL and propose AuxMix, an algorithm that leverages self-supervised learning tasks to learn generic features in order to mask auxiliary data that are not semantically similar to the labeled set. We also propose to regularize learning by maximizing the predicted entropy for dissimilar auxiliary samples. We show an improvement of 5% over existing baselines on a ResNet-50 model when trained on CIFAR10 dataset with 4k labeled samples and all unlabeled data is drawn from the Tiny-ImageNet dataset. We report competitive results on several datasets and conduct ablation studies.
abstract_zh: 当标记数据稀缺但未标记数据丰富时，半监督学习（SSL）取得了长足的进步。至关重要的是，最近的工作假设此类未标记数据是从与标记数据相同的分布中提取的。在这项工作中，我们展示了最先进的 SSL 算法在存在未标记辅助数据的情况下性能会下降，这些数据不一定具有与标记集相同的类分布。我们将这个问题称为 Auxiliary-SSL 并提出 AuxMix，这是一种利用自监督学习任务来学习通用特征的算法，以掩盖在语义上与标记集不相似的辅助数据。我们还建议通过最大化不同辅助样本的预测熵来规范学习。我们发现，在使用 4k 标记样本的 CIFAR10 数据集上进行训练时，ResNet-50 模型比现有基线提高了 5%，并且所有未标记数据均来自 Tiny-ImageNet 数据集。我们报告多个数据集的竞争结果并进行消融研究。

AI.tldr: 本文提出AuxMix算法，解决半监督学习中未标记辅助数据分布与标记数据不一致的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有半监督学习方法假设未标记数据与标记数据同分布，但实际应用中辅助数据可能具有不同类别分布，导致性能下降。
AI.method: 通过自监督学习任务学习通用特征以过滤不相关辅助数据，并对不相似样本进行预测熵最大化正则化。
AI.result: 在CIFAR10数据集上，使用4k标记样本和Tiny-ImageNet未标记数据时，ResNet-50模型性能较基线提升5%。
AI.conclusion: AuxMix通过特征学习与熵正则化有效处理分布差异，验证了其在多种数据集上的竞争力。

authors_text: Rudy Semola,Vincenzo Lomonaco,Davide Bacciu
title: Continual-Learning-as-a-Service (CLaaS): On-Demand Efficient Adaptation of Predictive Models
title_zh: 持续学习即服务 (CLaaS)：预测模型的按需高效适应
abstract: Predictive machine learning models nowadays are often updated in a stateless and expensive way. The two main future trends for companies that want to build machine learning-based applications and systems are real-time inference and continual updating. Unfortunately, both trends require a mature infrastructure that is hard and costly to realize on-premise. This paper defines a novel software service and model delivery infrastructure termed Continual Learning-as-a-Service (CLaaS) to address these issues. Specifically, it embraces continual machine learning and continuous integration techniques. It provides support for model updating and validation tools for data scientists without an on-premise solution and in an efficient, stateful and easy-to-use manner. Finally, this CL model service is easy to encapsulate in any machine learning infrastructure or cloud system. This paper presents the design and implementation of a CLaaS instantiation, called LiquidBrain, evaluated in two real-world scenarios. The former is a robotic object recognition setting using the CORe50 dataset while the latter is a named category and attribute prediction using the DeepFashion-C dataset in the fashion domain. Our preliminary results suggest the usability and efficiency of the Continual Learning model services and the effectiveness of the solution in addressing real-world use-cases regardless of where the computation happens in the continuum Edge-Cloud.
summary: Predictive machine learning models nowadays are often updated in a stateless and expensive way. The two main future trends for companies that want to build machine learning-based applications and systems are real-time inference and continual updating. Unfortunately, both trends require a mature infrastructure that is hard and costly to realize on-premise. This paper defines a novel software service and model delivery infrastructure termed Continual Learning-as-a-Service (CLaaS) to address these issues. Specifically, it embraces continual machine learning and continuous integration techniques. It provides support for model updating and validation tools for data scientists without an on-premise solution and in an efficient, stateful and easy-to-use manner. Finally, this CL model service is easy to encapsulate in any machine learning infrastructure or cloud system. This paper presents the design and implementation of a CLaaS instantiation, called LiquidBrain, evaluated in two real-world scenarios. The former is a robotic object recognition setting using the CORe50 dataset while the latter is a named category and attribute prediction using the DeepFashion-C dataset in the fashion domain. Our preliminary results suggest the usability and efficiency of the Continual Learning model services and the effectiveness of the solution in addressing real-world use-cases regardless of where the computation happens in the continuum Edge-Cloud.
abstract_zh: 如今的预测机器学习模型通常以无状态且昂贵的方式更新。对于想要构建基于机器学习的应用程序和系统的公司来说，未来的两个主要趋势是实时推理和持续更新。不幸的是，这两种趋势都需要成熟的基础设施，而在本地实现这一基础设施既困难又昂贵。本文定义了一种新颖的软件服务和模型交付基础设施，称为持续学习即服务（CLaaS）来解决这些问题。具体来说，它包含持续机器学习和持续集成技术。它以高效、有状态且易于使用的方式为无需本地解决方案的数据科学家提供模型更新和验证工具的支持。最后，这个 CL 模型服务很容易封装在任何机器学习基础设施或云系统中。本文介绍了名为 LiquidBrain 的 CLaaS 实例的设计和实现，并在两个现实场景中进行了评估。前者是使用 CORe50 数据集的机器人对象识别设置，而后者是在时尚领域使用 DeepFashion-C 数据集的命名类别和属性预测。我们的初步结果表明，无论计算发生在连续体边缘云中的何处，持续学习模型服务的可用性和效率以及解决方案在解决实际用例方面的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Continual Learning-as-a-Service (CLaaS)的新型软件服务框架，旨在高效实现预测模型的按需持续更新，解决传统方法在实时推理和模型更新中的基础设施难题。
AI.motivation: 当前预测模型更新依赖昂贵且无状态的本地基础设施，难以满足实时推理和持续更新的工业需求，亟需一种高效、易用且跨平台的持续学习服务解决方案。
AI.method: 结合持续机器学习与持续集成技术，构建支持模型更新验证的CLaaS框架，并开发名为LiquidBrain的具体实现，通过边缘-云环境下的两个实际场景（机器人目标识别与时尚领域属性预测）进行评估。
AI.result: 实验验证了CLaaS在边缘和云环境中的可用性与效率，证明其能有效解决实际应用场景中的模型持续更新问题。
AI.conclusion: CLaaS框架通过软件服务形式实现了持续学习的高效部署，为工业界提供了灵活、可扩展的模型更新解决方案，适用于边缘-云计算环境中的多样化场景。

search_text: Continual-Learning-as-a-Service (CLaaS): On-Demand Efficient Adaptation of Predictive Models 持续学习即服务 (CLaaS)：预测模型的按需高效适应 Predictive machine learning models nowadays are often updated in a stateless and expensive way. The two main future trends for companies that want to build machine learning-based applications and systems are real-time inference and continual updating. Unfortunately, both trends require a mature infrastructure that is hard and costly to realize on-premise. This paper defines a novel software service and model delivery infrastructure termed Continual Learning-as-a-Service (CLaaS) to address these issues. Specifically, it embraces continual machine learning and continuous integration techniques. It provides support for model updating and validation tools for data scientists without an on-premise solution and in an efficient, stateful and easy-to-use manner. Finally, this CL model service is easy to encapsulate in any machine learning infrastructure or cloud system. This paper presents the design and implementation of a CLaaS instantiation, called LiquidBrain, evaluated in two real-world scenarios. The former is a robotic object recognition setting using the CORe50 dataset while the latter is a named category and attribute prediction using the DeepFashion-C dataset in the fashion domain. Our preliminary results suggest the usability and efficiency of the Continual Learning model services and the effectiveness of the solution in addressing real-world use-cases regardless of where the computation happens in the continuum Edge-Cloud. 如今的预测机器学习模型通常以无状态且昂贵的方式更新。对于想要构建基于机器学习的应用程序和系统的公司来说，未来的两个主要趋势是实时推理和持续更新。不幸的是，这两种趋势都需要成熟的基础设施，而在本地实现这一基础设施既困难又昂贵。本文定义了一种新颖的软件服务和模型交付基础设施，称为持续学习即服务（CLaaS）来解决这些问题。具体来说，它包含持续机器学习和持续集成技术。它以高效、有状态且易于使用的方式为无需本地解决方案的数据科学家提供模型更新和验证工具的支持。最后，这个 CL 模型服务很容易封装在任何机器学习基础设施或云系统中。本文介绍了名为 LiquidBrain 的 CLaaS 实例的设计和实现，并在两个现实场景中进行了评估。前者是使用 CORe50 数据集的机器人对象识别设置，而后者是在时尚领域使用 DeepFashion-C 数据集的命名类别和属性预测。我们的初步结果表明，无论计算发生在连续体边缘云中的何处，持续学习模型服务的可用性和效率以及解决方案在解决实际用例方面的有效性。 本文提出了一种名为Continual Learning-as-a-Service (CLaaS)的新型软件服务框架，旨在高效实现预测模型的按需持续更新，解决传统方法在实时推理和模型更新中的基础设施难题。 Rudy Semola,Vincenzo Lomonaco,Davide Bacciu cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.DC

authors_text: Bolun "Namir" Xia,Vipula D. Rawte,Mohammed J. Zaki,Aparna Gupta
title: FETILDA: An Effective Framework For Fin-tuned Embeddings For Long Financial Text Documents
title_zh: FETILDA：长金融文本文档的 Fin-tuned 嵌入的有效框架
abstract: Unstructured data, especially text, continues to grow rapidly in various domains. In particular, in the financial sphere, there is a wealth of accumulated unstructured financial data, such as the textual disclosure documents that companies submit on a regular basis to regulatory agencies, such as the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). These documents are typically very long and tend to contain valuable soft information about a company's performance. It is therefore of great interest to learn predictive models from these long textual documents, especially for forecasting numerical key performance indicators (KPIs). Whereas there has been a great progress in pre-trained language models (LMs) that learn from tremendously large corpora of textual data, they still struggle in terms of effective representations for long documents. Our work fills this critical need, namely how to develop better models to extract useful information from long textual documents and learn effective features that can leverage the soft financial and risk information for text regression (prediction) tasks. In this paper, we propose and implement a deep learning framework that splits long documents into chunks and utilizes pre-trained LMs to process and aggregate the chunks into vector representations, followed by self-attention to extract valuable document-level features. We evaluate our model on a collection of 10-K public disclosure reports from US banks, and another dataset of reports submitted by US companies. Overall, our framework outperforms strong baseline methods for textual modeling as well as a baseline regression model using only numerical data. Our work provides better insights into how utilizing pre-trained domain-specific and fine-tuned long-input LMs in representing long documents can improve the quality of representation of textual data, and therefore, help in improving predictive analyses.
summary: Unstructured data, especially text, continues to grow rapidly in various domains. In particular, in the financial sphere, there is a wealth of accumulated unstructured financial data, such as the textual disclosure documents that companies submit on a regular basis to regulatory agencies, such as the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). These documents are typically very long and tend to contain valuable soft information about a company's performance. It is therefore of great interest to learn predictive models from these long textual documents, especially for forecasting numerical key performance indicators (KPIs). Whereas there has been a great progress in pre-trained language models (LMs) that learn from tremendously large corpora of textual data, they still struggle in terms of effective representations for long documents. Our work fills this critical need, namely how to develop better models to extract useful information from long textual documents and learn effective features that can leverage the soft financial and risk information for text regression (prediction) tasks. In this paper, we propose and implement a deep learning framework that splits long documents into chunks and utilizes pre-trained LMs to process and aggregate the chunks into vector representations, followed by self-attention to extract valuable document-level features. We evaluate our model on a collection of 10-K public disclosure reports from US banks, and another dataset of reports submitted by US companies. Overall, our framework outperforms strong baseline methods for textual modeling as well as a baseline regression model using only numerical data. Our work provides better insights into how utilizing pre-trained domain-specific and fine-tuned long-input LMs in representing long documents can improve the quality of representation of textual data, and therefore, help in improving predictive analyses.
abstract_zh: 非结构化数据，尤其是文本，在各个领域持续快速增长。特别是在金融领域，积累了大量的非结构化金融数据，例如公司定期向美国证券交易委员会（SEC）等监管机构提交的文本披露文件。这些文件通常很长，并且往往包含有关公司业绩的有价值的软信息。因此，从这些长文本文档中学习预测模型非常有意义，特别是对于预测数字关键绩效指标（KPI）。尽管从巨大的文本数据语料库中学习的预训练语言模型（LM）已经取得了巨大进步，但它们在长文档的有效表示方面仍然存在困难。我们的工作满足了这一关键需求，即如何开发更好的模型来从长文本文档中提取有用信息，并学习可以利用软财务和风险信息进行文本回归（预测）任务的有效特征。在本文中，我们提出并实现了一个深度学习框架，该框架将长文档分割成块，并利用预先训练的 LM 来处理和聚合块为向量表示，然后通过自注意力来提取有价值的文档级特征。我们根据美国银行的 10-K 公开披露报告集合以及美国公司提交的另一个报告数据集来评估我们的模型。总体而言，我们的框架优于文本建模的强大基线方法以及仅使用数值数据的基线回归模型。我们的工作提供了更好的见解，让我们了解如何利用预先训练的特定领域和经过微调的长输入 LM 来表示长文档，从而提高文本数据表示的质量，从而有助于改进预测分析。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FETILDA框架，通过分割长文本并结合预训练语言模型与自注意力机制，提升从长金融文本中提取特征以预测关键绩效指标的能力。
AI.motivation: 金融领域存在大量长文本数据（如SEC披露文件），但现有预训练语言模型难以有效处理长文档，限制了从软信息中提取预测性特征的能力。
AI.method: 将长文档分块处理，利用预训练语言模型生成向量表示，通过自注意力机制提取文档级特征，并在金融数据集上验证效果。
AI.result: 在US银行10-K报告及公司披露数据集上，FETILDA优于文本建模基线和纯数值回归模型，证明其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过域适应和长输入优化的预训练模型可提升长文本表示质量，从而增强金融文本的预测分析效果。

authors_text: Conrad M Albrecht,Chenying Liu,Yi Wang,Levente Klein,Xiao Xiang Zhu
title: Monitoring Urban Forests from Auto-Generated Segmentation Maps
title_zh: 从自动生成的分割地图监测城市森林
comment: accepted for presentation and publication at IGARSS 2022
comments: accepted for presentation and publication at IGARSS 2022
abstract: We present and evaluate a weakly-supervised methodology to quantify the spatio-temporal distribution of urban forests based on remotely sensed data with close-to-zero human interaction. Successfully training machine learning models for semantic segmentation typically depends on the availability of high-quality labels. We evaluate the benefit of high-resolution, three-dimensional point cloud data (LiDAR) as source of noisy labels in order to train models for the localization of trees in orthophotos. As proof of concept we sense Hurricane Sandy's impact on urban forests in Coney Island, New York City (NYC) and reference it to less impacted urban space in Brooklyn, NYC.
summary: We present and evaluate a weakly-supervised methodology to quantify the spatio-temporal distribution of urban forests based on remotely sensed data with close-to-zero human interaction. Successfully training machine learning models for semantic segmentation typically depends on the availability of high-quality labels. We evaluate the benefit of high-resolution, three-dimensional point cloud data (LiDAR) as source of noisy labels in order to train models for the localization of trees in orthophotos. As proof of concept we sense Hurricane Sandy's impact on urban forests in Coney Island, New York City (NYC) and reference it to less impacted urban space in Brooklyn, NYC.
abstract_zh: 我们提出并评估了一种弱监督方法，以基于遥感数据和接近零的人类互动来量化城市森林的时空分布。成功训练用于语义分割的机器学习模型通常取决于高质量标签的可用性。我们评估了高分辨率三维点云数据（LiDAR）作为噪声标签源的好处，以便训练正射影像中树木定位的模型。作为概念验证，我们感知飓风桑迪对纽约市康尼岛城市森林的影响，并将其参考纽约市布鲁克林受影响较小的城市空间。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种弱监督方法，利用LiDAR点云数据生成分割地图以监测城市森林的时空分布。
AI.motivation: 传统语义分割模型依赖高质量人工标注，而获取此类标签成本高昂。本文旨在探索利用LiDAR数据作为噪声标签源，降低人工干预需求。
AI.method: 通过高分辨率LiDAR点云生成噪声标签训练分割模型，结合正射影像自动识别树木分布，并以飓风桑迪后的纽约市区域为案例进行验证。
AI.result: 成功生成城市森林分割地图，量化了康尼岛受飓风影响的森林变化，并与布鲁克林未受显著影响区域进行对比分析。
AI.conclusion: 该方法可有效减少人工标注依赖，为城市森林动态监测提供自动化解决方案，具有实际应用价值。
